title: Marketing Graph Profile
purpose: Represent a promotion system as graph objects for products, offers, audiences, channels, campaigns, public surfaces, content assets, metrics, evidence, hypotheses, constraints, handoffs and next actions.
non_goals: store raw CRM, ad or analytics data in canonical graph templates
invent product facts, prices, clients, results or legal claims
guarantee growth in leads, revenue, conversion, ranking or traffic
replace content, SEO, UX, testing, implementation or operations owner skills
readiness_model: Universal T-level readiness with marketing gates for source facts, audience/channel clarity, metric boundaries and companion-skill ownership.
evidence_model: Marketing claims require source refs or explicit hypothesis status; metrics are evidence snapshots with source, period and interpretation boundary.
quality_gates: facts, inferences, hypotheses and missing data are separated
offers and claims have source refs or are marked as hypotheses
metrics are evidence snapshots, not guaranteed outcomes
CRM, analytics and ad data are aggregated or referenced safely
companion owner is named for content, SEO, UX, testing, implementation and operations handoff
generated brief lists next executable batch
safety_policy: No invented customers, prices, legal claims or guaranteed growth; raw CRM, ad and analytics data stay outside canonical graph templates.
retention_policy: Profile stores reusable structure only; campaign facts, source registries, analytics evidence and channel tactics remain in the marketing skill or project graph.
federation_policy: Federated exports may include bounded aggregate summaries and handoff contracts, but must not expose raw CRM, ad account or analytics data.
migration_policy: Existing marketing skill graphs are migrated as candidates, reviewed by marketing owner, and accepted only after object/relation coverage and metric-boundary checks.
measurement_requirements: object coverage by reusable marketing role

basis: real marketing migration applied in two scoped batches
69 canonical objects and 86 canonical relations validated in ai-codex-skill-marketing
second apply resolved 16 evidence-required objects using concrete local evidence refs
benchmark-context second apply run completed with average_context_reduction 0.328128
limits: pilot-supported template, not broad universal superiority proof
semantic score still needs human confirmation for stronger claims
graph-first runtime enablement still requires runtime policy gate
